title: Green Ammonia and Biomethanol fuel MAritime Vessels
summary: GAMMA aims to retrofit an Ultramax bulk carrier and demonstrate safe integration of biomethanol, ammonia and fuel-cell related systems for emission reduction in commercial vessels.
why_useful: Strong project-level record with vessel retrofit, demonstration, alternative fuels and fuel value-chain testing.
evidence_snippet: The project goal is retrofitting an Ultramax bulk carrier for international routes and testing biomethanol/NH3 fuel systems.
hero_image_caption: GAMMA project visual from RINA project page
hero_image_rights_note: Use as linked preview with source attribution unless reuse rights are confirmed.

title: Dynamic navigation and port call optimisation in real time
summary: DYNAPORT develops optimisation and coordination tools for ports and ships to reduce fuel consumption and improve port efficiency by at least 10%.
why_useful: Directly aligned with smart ports, emissions reduction and operational optimisation.
evidence_snippet: The project aims to reduce fuel consumption and enhance port efficiency by at least 10%.
hero_image_caption: Container vessel image used on DYNAPORT project website
hero_image_rights_note: Likely licensed/stock image. Use as linked preview only unless rights are confirmed.

title: Integrated Digital Twin Framework to Enable Green Ship Operations Towards Zero Emission Vessels
summary: TwinShip develops an open-source digital platform and digital-twin enabled decision support system for green ship operations and emission reduction.
why_useful: Combines digital twin, AI/ML, clean fuels and green ship operations; suitable for country and technology-area panels.
evidence_snippet: The platform is powered by domain knowledge-based machine learning and AI capabilities and targets major GHG reductions.
hero_image_rights_note: Use as linked preview with project source attribution unless reuse rights are confirmed.

title: Safe and Efficient Use of Sustainable Fuels in Maritime Transport Applications
country_or_region: EU / Greece coordinator; 11 partners from 6 countries
summary: SAFeCRAFT demonstrates sustainable alternative fuel technologies, including hydrogen, ammonia and LOHC-based options, for waterborne transport applications.
why_useful: Very strong practical R&D record with demo vessel, safety assessment, energy efficiency and techno-economic feasibility.
evidence_snippet: The project demonstrates four SAF-enabling technologies while utilising H2 on a demonstration vessel.

title: Flexible Tool Environment for Holistic Retrofits and Decarbonization of Ships
summary: FIT-HORIZONS focuses on holistic retrofits and ship decarbonisation tools involving ship designers, operators, technology developers, software developers and a classification society.
why_useful: Strong actionability because it involves industry operators, retrofit technology and classification.
evidence_snippet: The consortium includes ship designers, ship operators, retrofit technology developers, software developers, research organisations and a maritime cluster.

title: Demonstration of long-endurance intelligent multi-purpose autonomous vehicles for marine applications
summary: MERLIN deploys hydrogen-powered unmanned surface vessels and autonomous underwater vessels operating autonomously using AI algorithms.
why_useful: High-value autonomous marine robotics project with AI and hydrogen-powered vehicle signal.
evidence_snippet: MERLIN uses hydrogen fuel cells and renewable energy for USV/AUV autonomy with AI algorithms.

title: AUTOnomous small and FLEXible vessels
summary: AUTOFLEX develops small autonomous zero-emission vessels for inland waterway freight, focusing on narrow and shallow waterways.
why_useful: Clear autonomous vessel and zero-emission inland shipping application.
evidence_snippet: The project develops small, autonomous, zero-emission vessels optimized for energy efficiency and shallow waterways.
hero_image_rights_note: Use as linked preview with source attribution unless reuse rights are confirmed.

title: Open collaboration and open Digital Twin infrastructure for Green Smart Shipping
summary: DT4GS supports shipping companies in reducing CO2e through real-time configurable digital twins for ship and fleet operational performance optimisation.
why_useful: One of the clearest green-shipping digital twin projects; useful for AI/digitalisation country profiles.
evidence_snippet: The project aims to support up to 20% CO2e reduction by deploying digital twins in Living Labs.
hero_image_caption: DT4GS container ship icon/visual from project website
hero_image_rights_note: Small visual; use as linked preview only.

title: Safe, Efficient and Autonomous: Multimodal Library of European Shortsea and inland Solutions
summary: SEAMLESS focuses on safe, efficient and autonomous solutions for European short sea and inland waterway transport.
why_useful: Relevant to autonomous waterborne transport and multimodal logistics.
evidence_snippet: SEAMLESS develops automated, cost-effective and resilient waterborne freight feeder services.
hero_image_caption: SEAMLESS project logo from ISL project page

title: Sustainable Energy and Alternative Fuels from Advanced Intermediate Renewable Energy carrieR technology
summary: SEAFAIRER relates to sustainable energy and alternative fuels for maritime shipping.
why_useful: Useful alternative-fuels record with project/funding source; needs follow-up enrichment later.
evidence_snippet: CORDIS results mention decarbonisation of maritime shipping.

title: MARITIME JUST-IN-TIME OPTIMIZATION INTEROPERABLE PORT CALL AND VOYAGE OPTIMIZATION TOOL
summary: MISSION focuses on interoperable port call and voyage optimisation tools for maritime just-in-time operations.
why_useful: Useful for smart port and voyage optimisation category, especially operational decarbonisation.
evidence_snippet: MISSION develops a digital real-time optimisation and decision-support tool for port call operations.
hero_image_rights_note: SVG logo; use cautiously with attribution and verify display compatibility.

title: SUPPORTING MARITIME ALTERNATIVE FUEL PROJECTS
summary: SeaThrive supports maritime alternative fuel projects and coordination in the waterborne renewable and low-carbon fuel ecosystem.
why_useful: Good ecosystem/coordination source for alternative fuel projects, though less technology-specific.
evidence_snippet: Supports the EU renewable and low carbon fuel ecosystem for waterborne transport.

title: Securing Europe’s MARitime domain against threats through vessel-CONNECTed services
summary: MARCONNECT addresses maritime-domain threats using vessel-connected services.
why_useful: Useful security/cyber-adjacent maritime-domain project source, needs further source enrichment.
evidence_snippet: CORDIS lists MARCONNECT as securing Europe’s maritime domain against threats through vessel-connected services.

title: Physical AI-powered hybrid maritime agent for coastal monitoring and infrastructure inspection
summary: Angler contributes to autonomous maritime systems for monitoring ports, subsea cables, offshore renewable assets and coastal ecosystems.
why_useful: Very recent project and strongly relevant to maritime AI, autonomous systems and offshore inspection.
evidence_snippet: Angler supports monitoring of ports, subsea cables, offshore renewable assets and coastal ecosystems.

title: Million Nautical Mile Fuel Cell System
summary: MiNaMi develops a megawatt-scale PEM fuel cell system for maritime applications with focus on durability.
why_useful: Clear technology-development record for maritime fuel-cell systems.
evidence_snippet: MiNaMi develops a megawatt-scale PEM fuel cell with maritime application focus.

title: Multifuel SOFC system with Maritime Energy vectors
summary: FuelSOME establishes technological feasibility of a flexible, scalable multi-fuel SOFC system for long-distance maritime shipping.
why_useful: Specific energy-system technology for maritime decarbonisation.
evidence_snippet: FuelSOME focuses on a flexible multi-fuel SOFC energy generation system for long-distance shipping.

title: A Novel Smart System for the safe Management of Nautical operations
summary: SafePort collects real-time ship approach, meteorological and maritime data to support safe nautical operations.
why_useful: Older but useful smart-port operational safety record; archive/background unless follow-up is found.
evidence_snippet: The technology collects real-time information about ships approaching ports and maritime conditions.

title: Adaptive strategies for enhancing threat-agnostic resilience of port ecosystems
summary: PORTAL develops a framework for improving resilience of port infrastructure and socio-ecological systems against multi-hazard risks.
why_useful: Recent port resilience record; useful for security/resilience but less directly technology-product oriented.
evidence_snippet: PORTAL is a threat-agnostic framework for enhancing resilience of port infrastructure.

title: Sustainable technologies for future long distance shipping towards complete decarbonisation
summary: ENGIMMONIA promotes alternative fuels such as ammonia and clean energy technologies for future long-distance shipping decarbonisation.
why_useful: Strong H2020 background record for ammonia and clean-energy maritime technologies.
evidence_snippet: The project promotes alternative fuels like ammonia for maritime sector decarbonisation.

title: Centre of Excellence in Maritime Robotics and Technologies for Sustainable Blue Economy
summary: MARBLE aims to establish a centre of excellence in maritime robotics and technologies for sustainable blue economy.
why_useful: Useful institution/cluster-level project, rather than one technology alone.
evidence_snippet: MARBLE establishes a centre of excellence in maritime robotics and blue-economy technologies.

title: AUTONOMOUS FLYING SHIPS FOR INTER-ISLAND AND INLAND WATERS TRANSPORT
summary: AIRSHIP explores autonomous flying ships for inter-island and inland waters transport.
why_useful: Unusual autonomous transport concept; useful as an innovation/high-risk record.
evidence_snippet: CORDIS lists AIRSHIP as autonomous flying ships for inter-island and inland waters transport.

title: Smart Maritime and Underwater Guardian
summary: SMAUG uses autonomous subsurface drones and devices to enhance port and maritime security.
why_useful: Relevant to port security, underwater robotics and maritime situational awareness.
evidence_snippet: SMAUG uses autonomous subsurface drones and devices to enhance port security.

title: FAcilitate the development of unmanned Vehicles in the maritime sectOR
summary: FAVOR supports development of maritime unmanned vehicles, responding to human error and operational efficiency challenges in shipping and ports.
why_useful: Very relevant to unmanned maritime vehicles; useful for autonomous-vessel map layer.
evidence_snippet: Maritime unmanned vehicles reduce human error and improve operational efficiency.

title: SMART EUROPEAN SHIPBUILDING
summary: SEUS works on a digital framework to improve efficiency and competitiveness of European shipyards.
why_useful: Useful shipbuilding digitalisation record with industry relevance.
evidence_snippet: SEUS establishes a digital framework for European shipyards.

title: Sustainable Energy Transition in Shipping through Agreements, Industrial Leadership and Subsidies
summary: SET-SAILS studies how competition and industrial policy can support market uptake of future-proof alternative maritime fuel.
why_useful: Good policy/market uptake record, but less technology-demonstration oriented.
evidence_snippet: The project explores market uptake of future-proof alternative maritime fuel.
